title: Handlers, Middleware, Dependencies, and Application Architecture · Contract Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective composed handler and dependency boundary; retain the failing request and response, isolate the first causal server boundary, and keep a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in composed handler and dependency boundary and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use http.Handler and HandlerFunc as request-to-response boundaries while keeping response ownership and handler lifetime explicit.
Compose recovery, request ID, trust, authentication, authorization, limit, observation, and application middleware in a defended order.
Construct long-lived immutable dependencies at startup and inject narrow interfaces without service locators or per-request pool creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Response Single Owner
steps.instruction:
Order the Response Single Owner Go server HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Response Single Owner.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Response Single Owner assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Response Single Owner, keep Response Single Owner connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: A debug team handles case 1017 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Guarantee one response owner, stop after terminal writes, preserve error identity internally, and prevent double status or body writes.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Response Single Owner has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Build deterministic Go evidence to trace middleware order while keeping response ownership explicit; then make specification and runtime disagree on one nullable field and block release. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Guarantee one response owner, stop after terminal writes, preserve error identity internally, and prevent double status or body writes.

For Go server Order the regression proof · Debug Response Single Owner, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Calling WriteHeader again replaces the earlier status when a downstream error occurs.

options.text: Declare Go server method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the debug Response Single Owner case
Predict Go server resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the debug Response Single Owner case
Run the smallest deterministic Go server request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the debug Response Single Owner case
Inject one changed Go server failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the debug Response Single Owner case
